intro: Porting your codebase from JavaScript to Ruby swaps an asynchronous, event-driven scripting runtime for a pure object-oriented language focused on developer happiness and human-readable syntax. This path is commonly utilized when integrating with comprehensive backend framework platforms like Ruby on Rails. The conversion process repositions your execution pattern from an async event pipeline into a highly elegant object-driven model.

diffs.from: Dynamic types evaluated with lenient type coercion routines and prototype structure schemas.
diffs.to: Strong dynamic typing where everything is an object, strictly rejecting invalid cross-type calculations.

diffs.from: C-style brace scripting containing explicit return declarations and optional semicolon endings.
diffs.to: Highly concise, natural language layout omitting brackets, parentheses, and explicit return statements from block evaluations.

diffs.from: Engine-driven garbage collection optimized for short-duration variable scopes and UI rendering actions.
diffs.to: Mark-and-sweep garbage collection engines managing highly abstract runtime object allocations dynamically.

diffs.from: Native asynchronous execution queues processing task pipelines via promises.
diffs.to: Synchronous by design; concurrent execution requires explicit utilization of native threads, fibers, or background job runners.

diffs.from: Tailored for web platform APIs, browser layouts, and string conversions.
diffs.to: Deep structural toolsets designed for string handling, metadata transformations, and system management scripting tasks.

examples.title: Array Iteration Logic
examples.description: Transforming standard array iterations into clean, idiomatic Ruby block processing loops.

examples.title: Dynamic Class Layouts
examples.description: Converting public JavaScript constructors into unified object representations using implicit returns.

tips.title: Drop Explicit Returns
tips.body: Ruby automatically returns the evaluation result of the last line executed inside a block structure.

tips.title: Convert Scopes to Instance Variables
tips.body: Replace internal class references (this.prop) with explicit instance variable markers (@prop) inside your models.

tips.title: Utilize Ruby Blocks Safely
tips.body: Swap out standard arrow functions and lambda handlers for clean, native Ruby block expressions.

tips.title: Watch Variable Conversions
tips.body: Python and Ruby strictly block adding numbers to text strings; enforce explicit type casting using methods like '.to_s' or '.to_i'.

faqs.question: What is the equivalent of an Object literal in Ruby?
faqs.answer: Use Hash structures (e.g., { key: 'value' }) to manage flexible key-value data mappings safely.

faqs.question: How do I install external libraries?
faqs.answer: Instead of using npm, Ruby relies on the Bundler system alongside a Gemfile to download library extensions called 'gems'.

faqs.question: Is Ruby single-threaded like JavaScript?
faqs.answer: Ruby supports OS multi-threading, but its standard CRuby engine relies on a Global VM Lock (GVL) that restricts concurrent execution of Ruby bytecode to a single core.
